title: potentilla carniolica
intro: Potentilla carniolica, also known as the Carinthian cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species in the rose family Rosaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, thanks to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Potentilla carniolica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in herbal remedies for its potential antioxidant and immune-boosting properties.
